data.title: New Zealand Total Loans, Advances Rise to NZ$554.8 Billion in May
data.description:
New Zealand's total loans and advances rose to NZ$554.8 billion in May from NZ$553.13 billion in April, data from the Reserve Bank showed on Friday.

Core funding fell to NZ$494.59 billion, compared with NZ$496.77 billion month on month, with the core funding ratio slipping to over 89% from about 90%.

data.title: Future Vision for Health Training Leases New Branch in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Future Vision for Health Training Co. (SASE:9632) signed a lease contract with Social Development Bank - Jaddah 30 for a new branch in the Northern Region, Hail City, Saudi Arabia.

The one-year lease has a rental value of 25,000 Saudi riyals and is part of the health education company's strategic plans to expand its educational and health services across the country.

data.title: U.S. PE Firm I Squared Ahead Among Bidders for KKR's Singapore Logistics Firm Goodpack, Sources Say
South Korea's New Commercial Act to Provide Growth Momentum for Kospi — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea's new Commercial Act could provide further growth momentum for the benchmark Kospi, Nomura analyst Cindy Park writes in a note. The legislature's July 3 passage of a revision to the act could be "a meaningful starting point" to address the "Korea Discount"--a reference to the chronically low valuations of local companies compared with global peers--largely due to poor corporate governance, Park says. The amended act now includes expanding board members' fiduciary duties to all shareholders, mandating electronic voting for shareholders' meetings, and limiting the voting rights of the largest shareholders to 3% when electing audit committee members, she notes. The Kospi has risen around 30% this year, she adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: KKR Expects to Complete Goodpack Sale by November, Sources Say
I Squared Has Begun Due Diligence Exercise for Goodpack Valued at Up to $1.5B, Sources Say
Brookfield Asset Management, Apollo Global Management Among Others in Fray for Goodpack, Sources Say
Xinhuanet to Invest 15 Million Yuan in Partnership Fund
data.description:
Xinhuanet , through subsidiary Xinhua Net Venture Capital, will invest 15 million yuan into a partnership fund, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

The fund, Xinhua Net Shengshi Qingneng (Nantong) Equity Investment Fund Partnership (Limited Partnership), will have a total capital of 35 million yuan and a term of 7 years.

The fund will invest in Wison Clean Energy Technology Group, the Chinese state media company said.

data.title: Yomiuri: Japanese Food Maker Kewpie to Fully Acquire Aohata to Speed up Decision-Making
data.description:
By Kengo Tabe / Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Kewpie Corp., Japanese food maker known for its mayonnaise, announced Thursday it will acquire its consolidated subsidiary Aohata Corp. through a stock swap.

Amid a tough business environment due to rising costs of raw materials and labor, the move is aimed at accelerating decision-making processes, according to the company.

Aohata, established in 1932 by Kewpie founder Toichiro Nakashima, manufactures and sells jam.

Kewpie currently holds a 44.62% stake in Aohata and plans to acquire the remaining shares. Aohata is expected to be delisted from the stock exchange on Oct. 30.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-m0000126237-1

data.title: U.S. Private-Equity Firm I Squared Leads in Race for KKR's Singapore Logistics Asset
data.description:
(03:47 GMT) *U.S. PE Firm I Squared Ahead Among Bidders for KKR's Singapore Logistics Firm Goodpack, Sources Say
(03:47 GMT) *I Squared Has Begun Due Diligence Exercise for Goodpack Valued at Up to $1.5B, Sources Say
(03:47 GMT) *Brookfield Asset Management, Apollo Global Management Among Others in Fray for Goodpack, Sources Say
(03:47 GMT) *KKR Expects to Complete Goodpack Sale by November, Sources Say
(03:48 GMT) U.S. Private-Equity Firm I Squared Leads in Race for KKR's Singapore Logistics Asset

By P.R. Venkat

U.S. private-equity firm I Squared Capital has emerged as a leading contender to acquire KKR's Singapore-based supply chain and logistics company, valued at up to $1.5 billion, people familiar with the process said.

I Squared is conducting due diligence for KKR's logistics company, Goodpack, the people said. Other bidders for the asset include global investment firms such as Brookfield Asset Management and Apollo Global Management, they said.

Brookfield declined to comment. I Squared and Apollo didn't respond to requests for comment.

According to a tentative plan, the entire due diligence process will likely be completed by September, and a potential deal could be finalized by the end of November.

The process is still continuing, and there is no guarantee that a deal will be reached, one of the people said.

KKR had acquired Singapore-listed Goodpack in 2014 for more than $1.0 billion and subsequently delisted the company.

One of the people had said earlier that the private-equity firm considered a potential sale in 2020, but it didn't materialize because of the pandemic and volatile market conditions.

Goodpack, established in 1980, bills itself as the world's first and largest provider of reusable pallet-sized intermediate bulk containers on a pay-per-use basis for companies transporting valuable payloads globally by road, rail or sea.

The company makes containers and boxes for the chemicals, food, automotive and rubber industries. These containers are used as alternatives to wooden boxes and metal drums. Goodpack's containers are used in more than 70 countries, and it has operations in South Africa, Asia, the Middle East, and North and South America.

KKR, which had $664 billion in assets under management as of end-March, has been actively making deals in recent years, particularly in the Asia-Pacific region. It has been rebalancing its portfolio across asset classes through a combination of investments and divestitures.

Some of its recent deals included the acquisition of ProTen, one of Australia's largest agricultural infrastructure businesses. Last month, the company said it was providing $600.0 million in financing to Indian conglomerate Manipal Group. KKR in June also announced the sale of its nearly 47% stake in India-listed J.B. Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals for $1.4 billion.

Write to P.R. Venkat at venkat.pr@wsj.com

data.title: Yanchang Petroleum Says Court Terminates Unit's Bankruptcy Proceeding; Shares Gain 3%
data.description:
Yanchang Petroleum International said the bankruptcy process for its 51%-owned subsidiary, Yanchang Petroleum (Zhejiang FTZ), has formally concluded, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the company were up about 3% in Friday morning trade.

The Intermediate People's Court ruled on June 27 to terminate the subsidiary's reorganization and officially declared the subsidiary bankrupt.

The court then accepted a petition from the appointed administrator, Guangdong Oriental Kunlun Law Firm, to end the bankruptcy proceedings, effective June 30, according to the filing.

Yanchang said the unit had already been deconsolidated from its financial statements as of end-2023, and the development is not expected to have any significant impact on the group's current operations or financial position.

data.title: Ringgit May Strengthen on Hopes for Lower Tariff, Unchanged Interest Rates — Market Talk
data.description: Ringgit may strengthen toward 4.20 versus the U.S. dollar next week, Kenanga IB economists say in a note. Ringgit may be supported by expectations that Bank Negara Malaysia will keep rates unchanged at 3.00% amid stable growth and potential inflation risks. Possible reciprocal tariffs of 10% affecting around 100 countries, likely including Malaysia, could support the ringgit as the rate is lower than the proposed 24%, they note. The trade and structural shifts, alongside steady domestic fundamentals, could attract foreign inflows, thereby bolstering ringgit sentiment, they add. Kenanga expects USD/MYR to face resistance at 4.24 and find support at 4.21 in the short term. USD/MYR is 0.2% higher at 4.2295.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Indian shares open flat as Jane Street ban, trade deal caution weigh
data.description:
Indian shares opened flat on Friday stayed on the sidelines ahead of a possible India-U.S. trade deal and digested the market regulator's ban on U.S. firm Jane Street over alleged manipulation in derivatives trading.

The Nifty 50 rose 0.09% to 25,428.85 points and the BSE Sensex added 0.08% to 83,306.81, as of 9:15 a.m. IST.

Twelve of the 13 major sectors advanced at the open, although the gains were marginal. The broader, small-caps and mid-caps traded flat.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has passed an interim order on Jane Street's alleged manipulation through positions taken in equities derivatives.

SEBI's actions come as half a dozen global trading firms, including Citadel Securities, IMC Trading, Millennium and Optiver, are ratcheting up their presence in India's booming derivatives markets.

Meanwhile, investors are awaiting a potential trade deal between India and the U.S. ahead of President Donald Trump's July 9 deadline for punitive reciprocal tariffs.

data.title: Provaris Energy Raises $1.08m via Placement to Advance Unique Gas Compression Technology
data.description: Hydrogen and carbon dioxide storage technology specialist Provaris Energy has raised $1.08 million to support its continued expansion into Europe. The raising received strong institutional, sophisticated, and professional investor support with 83 million new fully paid ordinary shares to be issued at $0.013 per share, and included strong participation from Provaris directors. The transaction will settle in a single tranche with the new shares, which will rank equally with the company’s existing fully paid ordinary shares, to be issued sometime in early July. Shipping and Storage Solutions Chief executive officer Martin Carolan said Provaris would apply the proceeds from the raising toward advancing the company’s priority hydrogen and carbon dioxide storage and marine transportation business development activities in Europe, including its proprietary hydrogen prototype tank program in Norway. “Provaris is pleased to welcome new shareholders who have supported this capital raising and we thank our existing holders for their continued confidence,” Mr Carolan said. “We are also encouraged by progress in completing the liquid CO2 tank design phase funded through our joint development agreement with Yinson Production.” “The new tank design will address market demand for specialised maritime and offshore storage solutions, supporting large-scale carbon capture and storage to comply with Europe’s increasing regulations on industrial supply chains.” K Line Agreement Provaris executed a memorandum of understanding in late June with global shipping leader Kawasaki Kisen Kaisha (‘K’ LINE), a deal the company sees as a significant step toward the commercialisation of its shipping and offshore storage solutions, specifically its H2Neo carrier and H2Leo barge. “The funds raised will support ongoing H2Neo technical milestones in parallel with our partnership with ‘K’ LINE to support the commercialisation of Provaris’ hydrogen carriers for cost-effective storage and marine transport solutions for hydrogen in Europe,” Mr Carolan said. Analysts have forecast that Europe will require 7 million tonnes of low-carbon hydrogen, with Germany planning to import up to 70 per cent of its supply.

data.title: Australia's WA Kaolin jumps on record sales in June
data.description:
** WA Kaolin jumps as much as 15% to A$0.046, highest since May 14

** Stock set for best day since April 29

** Mineral extraction firm posts record sales of 4,677 tons in June worth ~A$1 mln ($663,065)

** Says forward orders as of July 1 are 12,911 tons, of which 5,562 tons are expected to be delivered in July

** Stock sees busiest trading session since March 27, with ~719,700 shares traded - ~10x the 30-day average

** WAK last up 10%, trims YTD losses to 7%

($1 = A$1.5232)

data.title: Market Chatter: Samyang Foods Begins Construction of First Overseas Plant in China; Shares Decline 4%
data.description:
Shares of Samyang Foods fell nearly 4% in Friday's morning trade despite reporting that it had started building its first overseas production plant in Jiaxing, China, to meet increasing demand.

According to the Yonhap News report on Thursday, the 201.4 billion-won facility will produce 840 million units annually and is expected to be completed by January 2027.

Once operational, Samyang's total annual Buldak production capacity will reach 3.5 billion units. The plant will mainly serve the Chinese market, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.1% Higher at 83306.81
Q & M Dental Launches, Prices SG$130 Million Worth of Bonds; Shares Down Nearly 4%
data.description:
Q & M Dental Group (Singapore) launched and priced its offering of SG$130 million worth of 3.95% bonds due 2028 under its SG$500 million multicurrency debt issuance program, according to a late Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the company were down nearly 4% in Friday morning trade.

The bonds are expected to be issued on July 10.

Net proceeds raised from the issuance will be used for general corporate purposes, financial acquisitions, working capital and for refinancing or borrowings.

DBS Bank, Overseas-Chinese Banking Corp. and United Overseas Bank have been appointed as joint lead managers of the bonds.

data.title: Info-Tech Systems Jumps 9% at Open on Singapore Debut
data.description:
Info-Tech Systems' shares opened at SG$0.95 when it debuted on the Singapore Exchange, up 9% from its IPO price of SG$0.87, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The shares were quoted in board lots of 100 shares.

The listing comes two days following the company's initial public offering where 24.85 million shares were offered and were 7.3 times oversubscribed.

data.title: Philippines Inflation Backs Further Policy Easing — Market Talk
data.description: Philippines headline inflation quickened from 1.3% on year in May to 1.4% in June, but stayed in line with the central bank's expectations, Goldman Sachs economists say. The uptick was driven by higher domestic fuel and electricity inflation, and partially offset by lower food inflation. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy, was unchanged at 2.2%. Goldman Sachs continues to expect that the Philippines central bank will cut its policy rate by 25 bps once more this year, likely in 3Q. That would bring the rate to 5.0%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0345 GMT - Philippines headline inflation quickened from 1.3% on year in May to 1.4% in June, but stayed in line with the central bank's expectations, Goldman Sachs economists say. The uptick was driven by higher domestic fuel and electricity inflation, and partially offset by lower food inflation. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy, was unchanged at 2.2%. Goldman Sachs continues to expect that the Philippines central bank will cut its policy rate by 25 bps once more this year, likely in 3Q. That would bring the rate to 5.0%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0314 GMT - The Singapore dollar consolidates against its U.S. counterpart during the Asian session amid concerns over tariffs. If U.S. President Trump unilaterally reimposes high tariffs on July 9 and/or on August 12, when his two tariff truces expire, foreign-exchange markets could become highly volatile, says CBA's Joseph Capurso in a research report. A "large currency reaction is possible because we consider market participants are complacent as illustrated by low implied volatility," says the head of International and Sustainable Economics. USD/SGD is little changed at 1.2746. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0259 GMT - A firmer-than-expected U.S. June jobs report leads Goldman Sachs economists to bring forward their Fed rate-cut expectations and lower the terminal rate, pointing to a lower range for U.S. yields across the curve. The economists now expect 10-year U.S. yields at 4.20% by end-2025, compared with their previous forecast of 4.50%, with a modestly steeper curve being the likely result. The economists maintain their European and U.K. rate forecasts. GS forecasts Bund yields to reach 2.80% later in the year before surpassing 3% in 2026, as near-term risks around trade uncertainty eventually give way to upside risks due to German fiscal expansion. Meanwhile, the Bank of England's path is unlikely to be affected by the rise in country risk premium, as front-end longs remain relatively well protected, GS says. (jihye.lee@wsj.com)

0217 GMT - Vietnam's trade deal with the U.S. brightens its outlook, say analysts at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. They reckon Vietnam managed to clinch a deal faster than others because it sought relatively lower tariffs rather than insisting on zero, and offered a major market-opening package. Despite the agreed-upon 20% levy, Vietnam's exports are set to keep growing steadily, BMI says. Vietnam's cost competitiveness will likely keep production from relocating to other economies, and BMI expects foreign direct investment to continue flowing. The certainty the deal provides will assure U.S. investors that Vietnam can still be part of their supply chains, and Chinese firms will still be incentivized to move production to Vietnam. BMI now thinks Vietnam's 2025 GDP growth could top its forecast of 6.4%. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0157 GMT - Japan's wage growth will likely continue next year as the final tally of this year's wage negotiations released by the country's largest labor union group showed the biggest pay increase in 34 years, Goldman Sachs economists say. While policymakers have expressed concerns that trade uncertainty may weigh on wage growth, the results showed that labor shortages are forcing companies to keep increasing pay to secure workers, the economists say. "Even if weaker corporate earnings were to put downward pressure on wage increases in next year's shunto negotiations, we believe the impact would be limited," they say. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0135 GMT - Asian currencies may face renewed volatility on a shift in focus to tariff-related developments, MUFG Bank's Lloyd Chan says in a research report. The risk of higher U.S. reciprocal tariffs looms, the senior currency analyst says. Notably, the Korean won, which is frequently used as a proxy hedge for global risk sentiment, fell 0.6% against the U.S. dollar in Thursday's session, Chan says. A breakthrough in U.S.-Korea trade talks appear to remain slim for now, and the South Korean government plans to ask for an extension of the tariff pause, Chan adds. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,366.30, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0046 GMT - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in the morning session amid mixed signals. On the one hand, the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report released overnight was much stronger than consensus, NAB's Tapas Strickland says in a commentary. On the other hand, however, pricing for Fed rate cuts was trimmed, with now 51.4bps worth of cuts for 2025 versus 65.0bps on Wednesday, the head of Market Economics says. "As for meeting timing, a September cut is priced at 18.1bps (or 72% priced) from 29.1bps on Wednesday," Strickland adds. USD/KRW is up 0.2% at 1,366.50, and AUD/USD is 0.1% higher to 0.6573, LSEG data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0018 GMT - JGBs edge lower in the morning Tokyo session, tracking overnight price declines in U.S. Treasurys. However, uncertainty over the BOJ rate-hike outlook has increased as the U.S.-Japan trade-talk deadline on July 9 approaches, two members of Barclays' FICC Research say. "If the deadline is reached without any significant progress and if tariff hikes or other measures are actually imposed, BOJ rate hike expectations could recede," the members say in a research report. This may lead to downward pressure on yields, mainly on the front end, and an increase in volatility and selling pressure in the super-long sector, they add. The 10-year JGB yield is up 1.5bps at 1.455%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0013 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher in early trade after solid U.S. jobs data boost optimism over the U.S. economic outlook. Bank and electronics stocks are leading the gains. Mizuho Financial Group is up 1.7% and Advantest is 1.8% higher. USD/JPY is at 144.70, up from 143.89 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any developments related to U.S. tariffs as well as domestic policymaking ahead of the upper-house election later this month. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.3% at 39920.82. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0004 GMT - The Reserve Bank of Australia has a case to cut interest rates next week, but it's likely to be cautious about the strength of the job market. Here, the outcomes are still strong, says Paul Bloxham, chief economist at HSBC. The unemployment rate has continued to track sideways, and it remains the case that wage growth is still running too quickly, given that productivity is falling, he adds. Still, Bloxham sees this as unlikely to stand in the way of a cut in July, but it should leave the RBA cautious. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

2341 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise after solid U.S. jobs data boosts optimism over the U.S. economic outlook. A weaker yen may also support the market. Nikkei futures are up 0.7% at 39975 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 144.79, up from 143.89 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. tariffs as well as domestic policymaking ahead of the upper-house election later this month. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 39785.90 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1846 GMT - Australia's household spending indicator for May at 0130 GMT will attract a lot of attention following recent retail activity indicators suggesting consumers are lacking the confidence needed to boost consumption. A weak HSI, which is a broader measure of spending than the monthly retail sales data, will further support market pricing for an interest rate cut next week. The RBA is expected to cut interest rates by further 25 basis points, but there's also an argument that policy makers may wait until August when they will have seen the latest quarterly CPI numbers. (james.glynn@wsj.com; X @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Future Vision for Health Training Signs One-year Lease for New Saudi Branch
data.description:
Future Vision for Health Training Co. (SASE:9632) signed a one-year lease with Social Development Bank - Jaddah 30 for a new branch in the Al-Ahsa Governorate, Saudi Arabia.

The health education company said Thursday the leased space will have a rental value of 19,900 Saudi riyals and will be used to provide accredited training programs.

data.title: Reinet Investments to Exit Specialist UK Insurer Pension Insurance
data.description:
Reinet Investments (, RNI.JO) said Thursday it agreed to sell all of its 49.5% shareholding in Pension Insurance Corp. Group under a deal that values the specialist UK insurer at 5.7 billion pounds sterling.

The sale will be carried out as part of a larger transaction wherein the investment manager, through Reinet Fund, and other shareholders are exiting Pension Insurance. Pan-European savings and retirement services group Athora is purchasing the insurer.

Other sellers include the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, CVC Capital Partners , and HPS Investment Partners. Completion is expected in early 2026, subject to regulatory approval.

Reinet said Reinet Fund will redirect proceeds from the sale to its investment initiatives.

data.title: Bitcoin must hold above $108K or risk a bearish spiral
data.description:
Bitcoin needs to keep its momentum as it approaches its all-time high, as a drop back to $108,000 could trigger a bearish downtrend, according to a trader.

“You don’t want to see this deviate back below $108K again at this point,” crypto analyst Daan Crypto Trades said on Thursday, adding that Bitcoin (BTC) is “attempting a breakout.”

Bitcoin has $2.67 billion in longs at risk

“Any closes up around this $110K region would be good,” he added. Bitcoin reached $110,498 on Thursday but has since retraced to $109,250, according to CoinMarketCap data. It is approximately 2.5% below its all-time high of $111,970.

Daan’s chart analysis indicates that if Bitcoin falls back toward $108,000 — a level it only recently regained on Wednesday — it could trigger a bearish downtrend, potentially pushing the price below $100,000 and possibly down as far as $96,000.

Bitcoin hasn’t dropped below $100,000 since June 22, when it briefly touched $98,900 amid the conflict between Israel and Iran. A move back below this level would wipe approximately $2.67 billion in long positions, according to CoinGlass data.

Several other analysts appear confident in a Bitcoin uptrend. Crypto analyst Miles Deutscher said, “It’s very hard to be bearish here.” 

Meanwhile, crypto trader CryptoFayz explained using a chart that if Bitcoin does break its current all-time high of $111,960, it could continue its uptrend to $116,000. 

Similarly, 10x Research’s Markus Thielen told Cointelegraph on Thursday that this price level could be reached by the end of July, citing strong inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs, uncertainty around the US Federal Reserve, and the rapid decline of Bitcoin supply on crypto exchanges.

Bitcoin consolidation phases are different this cycle

However, Daan said that Bitcoin’s current consolidation looks slightly different from earlier ones in this cycle. “It is still following the same pattern where it stalls, deviates below, retakes the range and then grinds higher,” he said, adding that it is missing “the actual breakout and continuation.”

McKay Research founder James McKay said, “The longer we have these periodic, multimonth consolidations, the more it’s going to take us off the beaten [path] with respect to the typical four-year cyclical behaviour.”

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Zangge Mining Unit Secures Construction Permit for Tibet Lithium Project
data.description:
Zangge Mining controlled entity Mamicuo Mining has received a construction permit for a lithium-boron extraction project at the Mamicuo Salt Lake in Gêrzê County, Tibet.

Construction at the 106,888-square-meter project is set from July 2025 to October 2026, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the miner were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Global Bio-chem Says Supplier Debt Restructuring on Track; Shares Slide 3%
data.description:
Global Bio-chem Technology said its supplier debt restructuring is progressing, with the initial phase involving a debt-to-equity swap in subsidiary Century Dacheng now completed, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The supplier creditors now hold approximately 28.98% equity interest in Century Dacheng following the transaction.

Shares of the company were down about 3% in Friday morning trade.

A second-stage equity swap, in which Global Bio-chem will issue new shares in exchange for this stake, is expected to begin in mid-July, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals.

In parallel, 33 remaining supplier creditors have agreed to settle about 50.5 million yuan in payables at a discounted value of 34.5 million yuan.

Global Bio-chem expects to record a net gain of no less than HK$17 million from the debt discounts. It is also negotiating similar terms with other suppliers that have not yet joined the restructuring arrangement, according to the filing.

data.title: G8 Education Shares Hit Lowest Since August 2020 After Sexual Assault Charges Against Former Employee
data.description:
G8 Education's shares hit their lowest since August 2020 on Friday, weighed down by the company's Wednesday announcement about sexual assault charges against a former employee.

The company's shares were down nearly 6% in recent Friday trade.

The Australian government is planning to cut funding from childcare centers that do not satisfy safety norms after recent cases of child sex abuse in the country, Reuters reported on Tuesday.

data.title: Bitcoin miner production falls in June on power curtailment, weather
data.description:
Several Bitcoin mining firms reported lowered BTC production in June, mainly due to power curtailment in Texas.

Riot Platforms produced 450 Bitcoin in the month, a 12% decline from May, when the firm mined 514 BTC, it stated in an announcement on Thursday.

Riot CEO Jason Les said the firm’s power strategy includes “economic curtailment” and voluntary participation in the Electric Reliability Council of Texas’s Four Coincident Peak (4CP) and other demand response programs. 

It “significantly contributes to grid stability while enhancing Riot’s competitive positioning,” he added. 

June marked the beginning of the ERCOT’s 4CP program, a tariff that captures demand charges in the market. It is designed to manage the periods of highest electricity demand during the months of June, July, August and September.

Large electricity consumers, such as Bitcoin miners, face transmission charges based on their usage during peak periods, so they can voluntarily curtail operations.

Riot also reported that it sold 397 BTC for $41.7 million and currently holds 19,273 Bitcoin.

Cipher production impacted

Cipher Mining reported this week that it had produced 160 BTC in June, sold 58 BTC, and holds 1,063 Bitcoin. 

The company stated that its June production numbers were impacted by deliberate curtailment as part of their “proactive 4CP avoidance strategy.”

“This approach allowed the company to avoid costly 4CP penalties and maintain its position as having some of the lowest power costs in the industry,” it stated. 

Cipher’s Black Pearl facility in Texas started contributing to production at the end of June, but the overall monthly production was reduced due to the strategic curtailment. 

MARA mining down 25%

MARA Holdings also reported a 25% reduction in production for June, with 211 Bitcoin mined compared to 282 the previous month. As of June 30, the company held a total of 49,940 BTC and did not sell any during the month. 

MARA CEO Fred Thiel said, “Following a record-breaking May, production in June came in lower, with 211 blocks won for the month.” 

He blamed the decline on “reduced uptime from weather-related curtailment” and the temporary deployment of older machines in its Garden City, Texas, facility while storm-related damage was being repaired. 

“Natural variability in block luck — an expected dynamic when operating our own mining pool — also contributed,” he said. 

CleanSpark bucks the trend

Meanwhile, CleanSpark increased its Bitcoin production by 6.7% in June, surpassing its mid-year hashrate target of 20 exahashes per second (EH/s). 

The firm produced 445 Bitcoin and only sold 8, bringing its total holdings to 6,591 Bitcoin as of the end of the month.
